•Purpose: Allele -Specific Copy Number Analysis 
of Tumours (ASCAT) infers allele -specific copy 
number profiles from SNP array or sequencing 
data while accounting for tumour aneuploidy 
and normal cell contamination.
•Key outputs: Estimates of tumour ploidy, 
aberrant ( tumour ) cell fraction (purity), and 
integer allele -specific copy numbers across the 
genome.
•Enables accurate identification of gains, losses, 
loss of heterozygosity (LOH) and copy -
number -neutral events that are confounded in 
raw data.
•Input signals: Uses LogR ratios (total copy 
number signal) and B -allele frequencies (BAF) 
(allelic imbalance) derived from SNP arrays or 
sequencing.
•Segmentation: Applies allele -specific 
piecewise constant fitting to segment and 
smooth LogR and BAF data.Workflow for ASCAT
•(a) Log2 R ratio (LRR): LRR values ofheterozygous autosomal SNPs (x -
axis: SNP index; y -axis: tumour vs non -malignant LRR). Red dots
represent individual SNPs; green dots indicate ASCAT segmentations .
•(b) B -allele frequency (BAF): BAF values for the same SNPs. Red dots
show individual SNP measurements ; green dots show ASCAT 
segmentation .
Cutcutache I, Wu AY, Suzuki Y, McPherson JR, LeiZ, Deng N, et al. Abundant copy -number loss ofCYCLOPS and STOP genes in 
gastric adenocarcinoma . Gastric Cancer . 2016;19:453 –465. Published 24 July 2015.

•Parameter estimation: Performs an iterative 
grid search to estimate tumour ploidy (ψₜ) 
and aberrant cell fraction ( ρ) by identifying 
solutions that best approximate non -negative 
integer allele copy numbers.
•The Objective: Find the solution where 
allele -specific copy numbers (NA, NB) are 
closest to integers.Solving for purity and ploidy
Best probability to be a correct estimation ofpurity and ploidy . The 
blue indicates a "hot" prediction and the red " cold "

Typical copy number profile in HGSC

•Tumour purity inferred from variant allele
frequency (VAF) ofTP53 mutations as an 
additional evidence for purity
•Adjusts for total copy number at the TP53 locus .
•Provides robust calibration for purity in 
aneuploid tumours , given the near -universal 
homozygous TP53 mutation in the HGSC cohortCorrecting ASCAT output
•Adjusts Log₂(R) ratios for normal cell contamination .
•Accounts for tumour purity and ploidy .
•Produces cleaner copy -number signals for 
segmentation and visualisation .
•Combines allele -specific segmentation with somatic
variant data.
•Distinguishes copy -loss LOH from copy -number -
neutral LOH .
•Relies on purity -corrected LogR and B -allele frequency
signals for accuracy .
Reference : Lavikka K, Oikkonen J, Li Y, Muranen T, Micoli G, Marchi G, Lahtinen A, Huhtinen K, Lehtonen 
R, Hietanen S, Hynninen J, Virtanen A, Hautaniemi S. Deciphering cancer genomes with GenomeSpy : a 
grammar -based visualization toolkit .GigaScience . 2024;13:giae040. doi:10.1093/ gigascience /giae040

Limitations: What the Data Does & Does NOT Tell Us
No Phasing
Short reads cannot phase
alleles across long
genomic distances.Subclonal Limits
ASCAT solves for the
dominant clone. Subclones 
are averaged out.
Not Amplified ≠Normal
Absence of amplification flag does
does not guarantee normal copy
number; requires baseline check.Epigenetics Invisible
Cannot detect methylation
silencing (e.g., BRCA1
promoter) via WES.
Deletion Ambiguity
?
Deciding on deletion is problematic.
Low signal can be true loss, low
low purity, or sampling error.
MAD
DO
Protein)
Function
Higher copy number means
more copies, not per 
seincreased gene
function or expression.
CN Results Relative
AbsoluteRelative
Copy number results are relative
estimates, not absolute cell counts.
Influenced by purity & ploidy.

What to take into account?
Gene type and alteration type
An amplification of tumour suppressor gene is unlikely to have an effect
Some genomic segments have normally variation
For example, genes of olfactory family have normally variation due to their function
There is normal copy number variation
Check GISTIC and COSMIC in analysis, or use literature to validate.
When in doubt, ask for help!

Visualising the Landscape:
GenomeSpy
•Flexible visualisation grammar: GenomeSpy uses 
a JSON -based grammar that allows users to freely 
compose visualisations from graphical building 
blocks (e.g., marks, scales) and map data 
attributes to visual channels such as colour and 
position, without relying on predefined templates.
•GPU -accelerated interactive rendering: The core 
library parses visualisation specifications and 
renders them using GPU acceleration, enabling 
smooth and responsive interactions such as 
zooming and panning.
•Scalable application for sample analysis: 
GenomeSpy App extends the core library to 
support analysis and interactive exploration of 
large sample collections 
Reference : Lavikka K, Oikkonen J, Li Y, Muranen T, Micoli G, Marchi G, Lahtinen A, Huhtinen K, Lehtonen 
R, Hietanen S, Hynninen J, Virtanen A, Hautaniemi S. Deciphering cancer genomes with GenomeSpy : a 
grammar -based visualization toolkit .GigaScience . 2024;13:giae040. doi:10.1093/ gigascience /giae040
